name: Simplify Tasks
description: A simple mobile application for managing tasks and boosting productivity.

current_prd.elevator_pitch: Codalio AI introduces Simplify Tasks, an intuitive mobile application designed to drastically reduce the time individuals spend on task management. Our app offers seamless task creation, organization, and tracking, empowering users to boost productivity, meet deadlines, and reclaim valuable time in their day.
current_prd.problem_statement: Individuals often struggle with managing their daily tasks, leading to disorganization, missed deadlines, and decreased productivity. Existing solutions are often too complex or lack essential features for simple, effective task management.
current_prd.solution: Simplify Tasks offers a streamlined, user-friendly platform for efficient task creation, organization, and tracking. With intuitive features like smart categorization, customizable due dates, and proactive reminders, Simplify Tasks empowers users to significantly reduce their task management time and enhance overall productivity.

key_features.feature_description: Users can easily create, edit, and delete tasks, setting titles, descriptions, and due dates.
Tasks can be categorized into lists (e.g., Work, Personal, Groceries) for better organization.
Users receive timely notifications for upcoming task deadlines.
A simple dashboard for administrators to manage users, monitor app performance, and view aggregated task data.
Backend APIs for task management, user authentication, and data synchronization.
Allows users to rapidly add tasks using natural language input, minimizing data entry time.

steps: User opens the app.
User taps the 'Add Task' button.
User enters task title, description, and due date.
User selects a category (optional).
User taps 'Save Task'.
System saves the task and displays it in the task list.
User opens the app.
User navigates to their task list.
User taps the checkbox next to a task.
System marks the task as complete and moves it to a 'Completed' section (or removes it from the active list).
user_flows.flow_name: User Marks a Task as Complete

steps: Admin logs into the Admin Dashboard.
Admin navigates to the 'Performance' section.
Admin views real-time metrics on server load, API response times, and database queries.
Admin identifies potential bottlenecks or issues.
User opens the app.
User taps the 'Quick Add' button/field.
User types a task description (e.g., 'Call John tomorrow 3pm').
System automatically parses the input for task title, due date, and time.
User confirms or adjusts parsed details.
User taps 'Save Task'.
System saves the task with parsed details and displays it in the task list.
user_flows.flow_name: User Utilizes Quick Add for a New Task

current_prd.success_metrics: Reduce user task management time by 20%. Increase monthly active users by 15% within 6 months. Achieve a 4.5-star rating on app stores within 12 months. Reduce customer support inquiries by 10% through intuitive design. Increase user retention by 5% quarter-over-quarter. Expand into new markets (e.g., Europe, Asia) within 2 years. Achieve profitability within 18 months. Integrate with 3rd party calendars (e.g., Google Calendar, Outlook). Reduce server costs by 10% through optimization.
current_prd.competitors: Any.do, Todoist, Microsoft To Do, Google Tasks, Apple Reminders.
current_prd.vision: To become the leading personal productivity app globally

target_audience.name: Individuals Seeking Organization
target_audience.pain_points: Forgetting tasks, feeling overwhelmed by responsibilities, lack of a centralized task management system.
target_audience.demographics: Ages 18-65, students, professionals, homemakers, anyone needing task organization.
target_audience.needs_and_goals: Efficient task management, reduced stress, improved productivity, clear overview of daily responsibilities.

target_audience.name: Small Business Owners/Team Leads
target_audience.pain_points: Difficulty in assigning and monitoring tasks, lack of visibility into team workload, missed project milestones.
target_audience.demographics: Small business owners, team leads, project managers, ages 25-55.
target_audience.needs_and_goals: Delegating tasks, tracking team progress, ensuring project deadlines are met, improving team collaboration.

target_audience.name: Students
target_audience.pain_points: Forgetting homework, missing project deadlines, feeling overwhelmed by coursework, difficulty prioritizing study tasks.
target_audience.demographics: Ages 16-24, high school and university students.
target_audience.needs_and_goals: Organizing assignments, managing study schedules, remembering deadlines, balancing academic and social life.

target_audience.name: Busy Professionals
target_audience.pain_points: Overwhelm from multiple responsibilities, difficulty in maintaining work-life balance, forgetting important deadlines, inefficient task switching.
target_audience.demographics: Professionals across various industries, ages 25-55, who manage demanding work schedules and personal commitments.
target_audience.needs_and_goals: Streamlined task management, effective prioritization, ability to separate work and personal tasks, quick task capture on-the-go.

target_audience.name: Team Collaborators
target_audience.pain_points: Miscommunication, duplicated efforts, lack of transparency in team tasks, difficulty coordinating across team members.
target_audience.demographics: Teams of 2-10 people, across various departments (e.g., marketing, engineering, design), ages 22-60.
target_audience.needs_and_goals: Shared task lists, real-time updates, clear task ownership, streamlined communication, project tracking.

phasing.rationale: This phase focuses on the essential task management features, allowing users to create, organize, and be reminded of tasks efficiently. This provides a basic but functional application that can be launched to early users.

This phase adds administrative capabilities for managing users and monitoring performance, along with a robust API to support future integrations and ensure data synchronization. This phase is demo-able for stakeholders and prepares for future enhancements.

current_prd.design_principles:
Simplicity: The interface should be clean, intuitive, and easy to navigate, minimizing cognitive load for users.
Efficiency: Users should be able to add, manage, and complete tasks quickly with minimal steps.
Clarity: Task information, due dates, and priorities should be clearly visible and understandable at a glance.
Accessibility: The app should be usable by individuals with diverse abilities, adhering to WCAG 2.1 AA guidelines.
Consistency: Maintain a unified design language, visual elements, and interaction patterns throughout the application.

user_personas.name: Sarah, The Busy Professional
user_personas.goals: Efficiently manage daily tasks, prioritize urgent items, track progress, and collaborate on shared tasks.
user_personas.pain_points: Forgetting tasks, difficulty prioritizing, overwhelming task lists, lack of clear overview.

user_personas.name: David, The Student
user_personas.goals: Organize assignments, study schedules, and extracurricular activities. Stay on top of deadlines.
user_personas.pain_points: Missing assignment due dates, procrastination, difficulty balancing academic and social life, disorganized notes.

user_personas.name: Emily, The Creative Freelancer
user_personas.goals: Manage multiple client projects, track deadlines, organize creative ideas, and maintain a work-life balance.
user_personas.pain_points: Juggling various projects, inconsistent workflow, forgetting client feedback, difficulty separating work and personal tasks.

user_personas.name: Maria, The Freelancer
user_personas.goals: Manage client projects, track billable hours, set personal goals, maintain a work-life balance.
user_personas.pain_points: Inconsistent workflow, difficulty separating work and personal tasks, managing multiple client expectations, tracking time accurately.

user_personas.name: John, The Project Manager
user_personas.goals: Oversee team projects, delegate tasks, track team progress, ensure project deadlines are met, facilitate team communication.
user_personas.pain_points: Lack of clear task delegation, difficulty monitoring team workload, missed project milestones, inefficient communication channels.

user_personas.name: Lisa, The Homemaker
user_personas.goals: Organize household chores, manage family appointments, plan meals, keep track of children's activities, reduce mental load.
user_personas.pain_points: Feeling overwhelmed by daily responsibilities, forgetting appointments, difficulty coordinating family schedules, lack of personal time.

user_personas.name: Alex, The Self-Improvement Enthusiast
user_personas.goals: Track personal goals (fitness, learning), build new habits, monitor progress, stay motivated.
user_personas.pain_points: Difficulty sticking to routines, losing track of multiple goals, lack of motivation, inconsistent progress tracking.

user_personas.name: Mark, The Small Business Owner
user_personas.goals: Manage staff schedules, track inventory, delegate daily tasks, ensure smooth operations, improve team communication.
user_personas.pain_points: Difficulty delegating effectively, inconsistent task completion by staff, lack of a centralized system for daily operations, time-consuming manual tracking.

steps.description: Opens the To-Do app.
Displays the main task list or a quick-add interface.
Taps on the 'Add New Task' button/icon.
Presents a task creation form/modal.
Enters task title, due date, priority, and any relevant notes.
Validates input and saves the new task.
Views the newly added task in her task list.
Confirms task creation and updates the task list.

user_journeys.context: Sarah needs to quickly add a new task to her list.
user_journeys.trigger: Receives a new assignment or remembers an important item.

steps.description: Opens the Simplify Tasks app.
Displays the main task list, possibly filtered by 'Today' or 'Upcoming'.
Scrolls through the task list to find a specific assignment.
Highlights the selected task.
Taps on the checkbox or 'Complete' button next to the task.
Marks the task as complete and moves it to a 'Completed' section or archives it.
Optionally, edits the task to add notes or change details.
Saves any edits and updates the task details.

user_journeys.context: David needs to review his upcoming assignments and mark one as complete.
user_journeys.trigger: End of the day review or completion of an assignment.

steps.description: Opens the Simplify Tasks app and navigates to the task details.
Displays the task details screen.
Taps on the 'Add Reminder' or 'Notification' option.
Presents options for reminder time (e.g., 15 mins before, custom date/time).
Selects a reminder time and frequency (e.g., one-time, recurring).
Saves the reminder settings and confirms the notification will be sent.
Sends a notification to the user at the specified time.

user_journeys.context: Emily wants to set a reminder for an important client meeting.
user_journeys.trigger: Creating a new task or reviewing an existing one that requires a timely action.

steps.description: Navigates to the 'Settings' or 'Integrations' section of the Simplify Tasks app.
Displays available integration options.
Selects 'Google Calendar' from the list of integrations.
Redirects the user to Google's authentication page.
Logs in to their Google account and grants Simplify Tasks permission to access their calendar.
Confirms successful integration and begins syncing tasks/events.
Views their Simplify Tasks tasks appearing in their Google Calendar, and potentially Google Calendar events appearing in Simplify Tasks.
Maintains a two-way sync (if implemented) or one-way sync based on user preferences.

user_journeys.context: John wants to integrate Simplify Tasks with his Google Calendar to see all his commitments in one place.
user_journeys.trigger: Desire to centralize schedule and task management.

steps.description: Opens the Simplify Tasks app and navigates to a project or shared list.
Displays the tasks within the selected project/list.
Creates a new task or selects an existing one to delegate.
Presents options to assign the task to a team member.
Selects a team member from a list of collaborators and assigns the task.
Notifies the assigned team member and updates the task with the assignee's name.
Monitors the task status (e.g., 'To Do', 'In Progress', 'Completed') from their dashboard.
Provides real-time updates on task progress and completion status.

user_journeys.context: Mark needs to delegate a task to a team member and track its progress.
user_journeys.trigger: Need to distribute workload or collaborate on a project.

current_prd.sitemap:
- Home / Dashboard (/) - Main landing page and overview of user's tasks and productivity.
  - My Day / Today (/today) - Displays tasks due today or overdue.
  - Upcoming / Calendar (/upcoming) - Displays upcoming tasks and events, potentially with calendar integration.
  - Lists / Projects (/lists) - Manages personal and shared task lists or projects.
  - Search (/search) - Allows users to search for tasks, notes, or projects.
  - Settings (/settings) - Application settings, profile management, and integrations.
current_prd.navigation_design:
Mobile Navigation: A bottom tab bar will provide quick access to core functionalities: 'My Day / Today', 'Lists / Projects', 'Upcoming / Calendar', and 'Search'. A central floating action button (FAB) will be used for 'Add Task'. A hamburger menu or user avatar in the top bar will lead to 'Settings' and other profile-related options.

Desktop Navigation: A persistent left-hand sidebar will house primary navigation links: 'Home / Dashboard', 'My Day / Today', 'Upcoming / Calendar', 'Lists / Projects', 'Search', and 'Settings'. A prominent 'Add Task' button will be available in the top right corner of the main content area. Breadcrumbs will be used for deeper navigation within 'Lists / Projects' to indicate the user's current location.

rows.name: Work
rows.description: Tasks related to my job and projects

rows.title: Finish Q1 report
rows.description: Compile sales numbers and finalize report for Q1 review.

rows.content: Please attach the latest spreadsheet before submitting.
Don't forget to pick up almond milk.

rows.description: Default minutes before due date to remind users if they have not set a custom reminder.

erDiagram
User {
  INT id
  TIMESTAMP created_at
  TIMESTAMP updated_at
}

  INT id
  TIMESTAMP created_at
  TIMESTAMP updated_at
}

  INT id
  TIMESTAMP created_at
  TIMESTAMP updated_at

  TIMESTAMP updated_at
}

  VARCHAR name
  TEXT description
  INT organization_id

  ENUM visibility
  INT position
  TIMESTAMP created_at
  TIMESTAMP updated_at
}

  VARCHAR title
  TEXT description
  DATE due_date
  TIME due_time

  TIMESTAMP completed_at

  INT organization_id
  INT user_id
  INT todo_list_id
  INT position
  JSON metadata
  TIMESTAMP created_at
  TIMESTAMP updated_at
}
Notification {
  INT id
  TIMESTAMP created_at
  TIMESTAMP updated_at
}

  INT id
  TIMESTAMP created_at
  TIMESTAMP updated_at
}

  TEXT content
  INT todo_item_id
  INT user_id
  INT organization_id
  TIMESTAMP created_at
  TIMESTAMP updated_at
}
StripeCustomer {
  INT id
  TIMESTAMP created_at
  TIMESTAMP updated_at
}
Reminder {
  INT todo_item_id
  INT user_id
  INT organization_id
  TIMESTAMP remind_at
  ENUM delivery_method
  VARCHAR recurrence
  INT occurrences
  DATE end_date
  TIMESTAMP snoozed_until
  BOOLEAN enabled
  TIMESTAMP created_at
  TIMESTAMP updated_at
}
NotificationSetting {
  INT user_id
  INT organization_id
  BOOLEAN reminders_enabled
  INT default_reminder_minutes_before
  ENUM default_delivery_method
  JSON channels
  VARCHAR sound
  BOOLEAN vibration_enabled
  TIMESTAMP created_at
  TIMESTAMP updated_at
}
SystemSetting {
  VARCHAR key
  JSON value
  TEXT description
  INT organization_id
  TIMESTAMP created_at
  TIMESTAMP updated_at
}
AuditLog {
  INT user_id
  INT organization_id
  VARCHAR target_model
  VARCHAR target_identifier
  VARCHAR action
  JSON details
  TIMESTAMP created_at
  TIMESTAMP updated_at
}

user_stories.title: Create New Task

user_story.i_want: to quickly create a new task
user_story.so_that: I can capture my to-do items without forgetting them

acceptance_criteria: User can input a task title.
User can optionally add a description.
User can optionally set a due date.
The task is saved successfully after creation.
User receives confirmation that the task has been added.

user_stories.title: Edit Existing Task

user_story.i_want: to modify the details of an existing task
user_story.so_that: I can keep my task list accurate and up-to-date

acceptance_criteria: User can select an existing task to edit.
User can change the task title.
User can update the task description.
User can modify the due date.
Changes are saved successfully.
User receives confirmation that the task has been updated.

user_stories.title: Delete a Task

user_story.i_want: to remove a task that is no longer relevant
user_story.so_that: my task list remains clean and focused

acceptance_criteria: User can select a task to delete.
User is prompted for confirmation before deletion.
Task is permanently removed from the list upon confirmation.
User receives confirmation that the task has been deleted.
Deleted tasks are no longer visible in any task lists.

user_stories.title: Create New Task Category

user_story.i_want: to create different categories or lists for my tasks
user_story.so_that: I can organize my tasks effectively (e.g., Work, Personal, Groceries)

acceptance_criteria: User can initiate the creation of a new category/list.
User can provide a name for the new category/list.
The new category/list is added to the available options.
User can view the newly created category/list.
Category names must be unique.

user_stories.title: Assign Task to Category

user_story.i_want: to assign a task to a specific category or list
user_story.so_that: I can keep my tasks organized and easily find them within their respective contexts

acceptance_criteria: User can select an existing task.
User can choose from a list of available categories/lists.
The selected task is moved to the chosen category/list.
User can see the task listed under its assigned category.
User can change a task's category after it has been assigned.

user_stories.title: API for Task Management

user_story.i_want: to have a well-documented API for task creation, retrieval, update, and deletion
user_story.so_that: I can build the user interface efficiently and reliably

acceptance_criteria: API endpoints exist for creating tasks.
API endpoints exist for retrieving single tasks and lists of tasks.
API endpoints exist for updating tasks.
API endpoints exist for deleting tasks.
API responses are consistent and well-structured (e.g., JSON).
API documentation is available and up-to-date.

user_stories.title: API for User Authentication and Data Synchronization

user_story.i_want: to implement secure user authentication and efficient data synchronization mechanisms
user_story.so_that: user data is protected and consistent across devices

acceptance_criteria: API endpoints support user registration.
API endpoints support user login and session management.
API endpoints support secure data synchronization for tasks and categories.
User data is encrypted in transit and at rest.
Authentication tokens are securely managed.
Data conflicts during synchronization are handled gracefully.

user_stories.title: Receive Task Deadline Reminders

user_story.i_want: to receive timely notifications for upcoming task deadlines
user_story.so_that: I don't miss important deadlines and can prioritize my work effectively

acceptance_criteria: User can enable/disable reminders for individual tasks.
User can set the reminder time (e.g., 1 hour before, 1 day before).
Notifications are delivered to the user's device (e.g., push notification, in-app alert).
Notifications clearly state the task title and due date.
User can snooze or dismiss a reminder.
Reminders are triggered even if the app is not actively open.

user_stories.title: Manage Notification Settings

user_story.i_want: to customize my notification preferences
user_story.so_that: I can control how and when I receive reminders without being overwhelmed

acceptance_criteria: User can access a dedicated notification settings section.
User can globally enable or disable all task reminders.
User can choose the type of notification (e.g., sound, vibration, silent).
User can set default reminder times for new tasks.
Changes to notification settings are saved and applied immediately.

user_stories.title: View App Performance

user_story.i_want: to view key metrics related to app performance
user_story.so_that: I can identify potential issues and ensure the app is running smoothly

acceptance_criteria: Admin can access a dashboard displaying app performance metrics (e.g., uptime, response times).
Dashboard shows real-time or near real-time data.
Metrics are presented in an easy-to-understand format (e.g., graphs, charts).
Admin can filter performance data by time range.
Alerts are triggered for critical performance deviations.

user_stories.title: Manage User Accounts

user_story.i_want: to manage user accounts within the application
user_story.so_that: I can ensure proper access control and address user-related issues

acceptance_criteria: Admin can view a list of all registered users.
Admin can search for specific users by criteria (e.g., username, email).
Admin can view detailed information for each user.
Admin can deactivate or reactivate user accounts.
Admin can reset user passwords.
Admin can delete user accounts (with confirmation).

user_stories.title: View Aggregated Task Data

user_story.i_want: to view aggregated data about tasks
user_story.so_that: I can understand overall usage patterns and identify trends

acceptance_criteria: Admin can access a section displaying aggregated task data.
Data includes total number of tasks created.
Data includes number of tasks completed vs. pending.
Data can be filtered by category or time period.
Data is presented visually (e.g., charts showing task completion rates over time).
Admin can export aggregated data for further analysis.

user_stories.title: Create a New Task

user_story.i_want: to quickly add a new task
user_story.so_that: I can keep track of my responsibilities.

acceptance_criteria: User can input a task title.
User can optionally add a description.
User can optionally set a due date.
The new task is saved successfully.

user_stories.title: Edit an Existing Task

user_story.i_want: to modify the details of an existing task
user_story.so_that: I can update my task information as needed.

acceptance_criteria: User can select an existing task to edit.
User can modify the task title.
User can modify the task description.
User can modify the task due date.
Changes to the task are saved successfully.

user_stories.title: Create a New Task Category

user_story.i_want: to organize my tasks into different categories
user_story.so_that: I can easily manage tasks related to specific areas of my life (e.g., Work, Personal, Groceries).

acceptance_criteria: User can access an option to create a new category.
User can input a name for the new category.
The new category is added to the list of available categories.
User can view the newly created category.

user_stories.title: Assign Task to a Category

user_story.i_want: to assign an existing task to a specific category
user_story.so_that: I can keep my tasks well-organized and easily filter them.

acceptance_criteria: User can select an existing task.
User can choose from a list of available categories.
The selected task is successfully assigned to the chosen category.
User can see the task listed under its assigned category.

user_stories.title: Set a Reminder for a Task

user_story.i_want: to set a reminder for a specific task
user_story.so_that: I don't miss important deadlines or forget to complete tasks.

acceptance_criteria: User can select a task and access reminder settings.
User can specify a date and time for the reminder.
User can choose the type of reminder (e.g., pop-up, sound).
The reminder is successfully saved and scheduled.

user_stories.title: Receive Deadline Notification

user_story.i_want: to receive timely notifications for upcoming task deadlines
user_story.so_that: I am aware of tasks that need my attention and can prioritize my work effectively.

acceptance_criteria: User receives a notification when a task's deadline is approaching.
The notification clearly displays the task title and due date.
User can dismiss the notification.
User can navigate to the task details directly from the notification.

user_stories.title: View User Activity

user_story.i_want: to monitor overall user activity and engagement
user_story.so_that: I can understand app usage patterns and identify areas for improvement.

acceptance_criteria: Admin can log in to the admin dashboard.
Admin can view a summary of active users.
Admin can see aggregated data on task creation and completion.
Admin can filter activity data by date range.

user_stories.title: Ensure API Stability

user_story.i_want: the backend API to be stable and reliable
user_story.so_that: the frontend application and other integrations can function without interruption.

acceptance_criteria: API endpoints respond within acceptable latency limits.
API handles expected load without degradation in performance.
Error handling is implemented for all API endpoints.
API documentation is up-to-date and accurate.

user_stories.title: Secure User Authentication via API

user_story.i_want: user authentication to be handled securely by the backend API
user_story.so_that: user data and accounts are protected from unauthorized access.

acceptance_criteria: API uses industry-standard authentication protocols (e.g., OAuth2, JWT).
User credentials are encrypted during transmission and storage.
API implements rate limiting for authentication attempts.
API provides secure token generation and validation.

user_stories.title: Quickly Add a Task with Natural Language

user_story.i_want: to rapidly add tasks using natural language input
user_story.so_that: I can minimize data entry time and capture tasks as soon as they come to mind

acceptance_criteria: User can type or speak a task like 'Buy groceries tomorrow at 5 PM'.
The system accurately parses the task title, due date, and time.
The task is automatically added to the default task list.
User receives a visual confirmation of the added task.
The quick add input field is easily accessible from the main screen.

user_stories.title: Ensure Robustness of Quick Add Task

user_story.i_want: the quick add task feature to be robust and reliable
user_story.so_that: users can consistently and quickly add tasks without errors or delays

acceptance_criteria: The natural language processing (NLP) engine accurately interprets various task formats.
The quick add functionality integrates seamlessly with the task creation API.
Error messages are user-friendly if natural language input cannot be parsed.
Performance of quick add is optimized for speed, adding tasks in under 1 second.
The system can handle concurrent quick add requests without degradation.

user_stories.title: Confirm Quick Added Task Details

user_story.i_want: to review and confirm the details of a task added via Quick Add
user_story.so_that: I can ensure accuracy and make any necessary adjustments before final saving

acceptance_criteria: After using Quick Add, a preview of the task details (title, due date, category) is displayed.
User can quickly edit any of the parsed details from the preview.
User can confirm the task to save it.
User can dismiss the preview without saving the task.
The preview clearly highlights any automatically parsed information (e.g., 'tomorrow' becoming a specific date).

user_stories.title: Mark Task as Complete

user_story.i_want: to mark a task as complete
user_story.so_that: I can track my progress and clear my to-do list

acceptance_criteria: User can select an incomplete task.
User can mark the task as complete with a single action (e.g., checkbox, swipe).
Completed tasks are visually distinguished from incomplete tasks (e.g., strikethrough, moved to a 'Completed' section).
User can view a list of completed tasks.
User can unmark a completed task as incomplete.
Completion status is synchronized across devices.

user_stories.title: Edit Task Category

user_story.i_want: to rename an existing task category
user_story.so_that: I can keep my categories organized and relevant to my changing needs

acceptance_criteria: User can select an existing task category.
User can rename the selected category.
The category name is updated across all associated tasks.
User receives confirmation that the category has been renamed.
Category names must remain unique after editing.

user_stories.title: Set Recurring Reminders

user_story.i_want: to set reminders that repeat regularly
user_story.so_that: I don't have to manually set reminders for recurring tasks

acceptance_criteria: User can set a reminder to repeat daily, weekly, monthly, or yearly.
User can specify an end date or a number of occurrences for recurring reminders.
The system generates and delivers reminders according to the recurring schedule.
User can view and modify the schedule of a recurring reminder.
User can stop a recurring reminder at any time.

user_stories.title: Configure System Settings

user_story.i_want: to configure global application settings
user_story.so_that: I can manage the overall behavior and policies of the Simplify Tasks application

acceptance_criteria: Admin can access a dedicated section for system settings.
Admin can configure global notification defaults (e.g., default reminder time).
Admin can manage user roles and permissions.
Admin can view and modify application-wide parameters.
Changes to system settings are applied immediately and logged for auditing.
Admin can revert to previous system settings if needed.

user_stories.title: API Error Logging and Monitoring

user_story.i_want: to have comprehensive error logging and monitoring for the API
user_story.so_that: I can quickly identify, diagnose, and resolve issues to maintain API reliability

acceptance_criteria: API logs all critical errors and warnings.
Logs include timestamps, error codes, and relevant request details.
Logs are stored securely and are accessible to authorized personnel.
Monitoring tools are integrated to alert on API errors and performance issues.
Error messages returned by the API are clear and informative for developers.
API logs do not expose sensitive user data.

user_stories.title: Categorize Tasks

user_story.i_want: to categorize my tasks into different lists
user_story.so_that: I can easily manage and prioritize my responsibilities

acceptance_criteria: User can create new categories (e.g., Work, Personal, Groceries).
User can assign a task to an existing category.
User can view tasks filtered by category.
User can rename an existing category.
User can delete a category, and associated tasks are either unassigned or moved to a default category.

user_stories.title: Receive Task Reminders

user_story.i_want: to receive timely reminders for my tasks
user_story.so_that: I don't miss important deadlines

acceptance_criteria: User receives a notification at the specified reminder time.
User can set multiple reminders for a single task.
User can snooze a reminder for a short period.
User can dismiss a reminder.
Notifications are delivered even when the app is closed.
User can customize notification sounds and vibration patterns.

user_stories.title: Quickly Add a Task

user_story.i_want: to quickly add a task using natural language
user_story.so_that: I can capture ideas and tasks without interrupting my workflow

acceptance_criteria: User can quickly add a task using a dedicated input field.
The system intelligently parses natural language input (e.g., 'Buy groceries tomorrow at 5 PM').
The system automatically assigns a due date and time if specified in natural language.
The quick-added task appears in the default task list.
User receives visual confirmation that the task was added.

user_stories.title: Manage Users and Monitor App Performance (Admin)

user_story.i_want: to manage users and monitor overall app performance
user_story.so_that: I can ensure the smooth operation and health of the Simplify Tasks application

acceptance_criteria: Admin can log in to a dedicated dashboard.
Admin can view a list of all registered users.
Admin can view aggregated statistics on app usage (e.g., total tasks created, active users).
Admin can search for specific users.
Admin can temporarily suspend or reactivate user accounts.
Admin can reset user passwords.

user_stories.title: Ensure Reliable Data Synchronization (Technical)

user_story.i_want: a robust and well-documented backend API
user_story.so_that: I can build and integrate client applications that reliably manage user data

acceptance_criteria: The API provides secure endpoints for user authentication (login, registration).
The API supports CRUD operations for tasks (Create, Read, Update, Delete).
The API ensures data consistency across all user devices.
The API is documented with clear specifications (e.g., OpenAPI/Swagger).
The API handles concurrent requests efficiently.
The API includes error handling and appropriate response codes.

user_stories.title: Edit and Delete Tasks

user_story.i_want: to be able to edit and delete existing tasks
user_story.so_that: I can keep my task list accurate and up-to-date

acceptance_criteria: User can select an existing task to edit.
User can modify the task title, description, and due date.
User can save the changes to the task.
User can delete a task from their list.
System prompts for confirmation before deleting a task.

current_prd.tam_description: Total Addressable Market for the Global Productivity Software and Personal Organization Tools market, encompassing all digital solutions designed to enhance individual and team efficiency, task management, and scheduling.
current_prd.tam_estimation_basis: Based on general estimates for the global productivity software market, which includes a wide range of tools from office suites to specialized apps.
current_prd.tam_source: General industry reports on global productivity software market size (e.g., Statista, Grand View Research).
current_prd.sam_description: Serviceable Available Market for individual and small team task management applications, specifically targeting simple, intuitive solutions for daily task organization, excluding complex enterprise-level project management software.
current_prd.sam_estimation_basis: Estimated 20 percent of the total productivity software market focusing on personal and small team task management, aligning with the 'Simple Todo App' positioning.
current_prd.sam_source: Market segmentation analysis of the broader productivity software market, focusing on the personal and small business segment.
current_prd.som_description: Serviceable Obtainable Market, representing the realistic market share Codalio AI's Simple Todo App can capture within the Serviceable Available Market over a 5-year horizon, considering the highly competitive landscape.
current_prd.som_estimation_basis: Conservative 1 percent market share estimate over 5 years, acknowledging the mature and competitive nature of the task management app market and the app's focus on simplicity.
current_prd.som_source: Competitive analysis of existing todo apps and typical market penetration rates for new entrants in crowded software categories.
current_prd.customer_type: Individuals (students, professionals, homemakers), small business owners/team leads
current_prd.pricing_model_basis: Freemium with a subscription for enhanced features (e.g., advanced reminders, collaboration, more categories, cloud sync)
current_prd.advantage_summary: Intuitive interface, user-friendly design, focus on simplicity and effectiveness, ensuring users stay productive and organized.
current_prd.project_manager_reasoning: The Project Definition is very comprehensive. All high-priority fields (Problem Statement, Key Features, Target Audience, Vision) are well-detailed and meet the content requirements. Medium-priority fields (Solution, User Flows, Competitors) are also well-covered. Low-priority fields (Elevator Pitch, Success Metrics) are present and sufficiently detailed. The 'Key Features' and 'Target Audience' sections are particularly strong with multiple, well-described elements. The 'Success Metrics' are specific and measurable. The overall document provides a clear and robust understanding of the project.
current_prd.designer_reasoning: The Design Document is strong, with all high-priority fields (User Journeys, Sitemap) being well-developed and detailed. The 'User Journeys' provide multiple scenarios with clear steps and actors. The 'Sitemap' is well-structured with children nodes. Medium-priority fields (User Personas, Navigation Design) are also very comprehensive, with numerous personas and detailed navigation descriptions for both mobile and desktop. The low-priority 'Design Principles' are clearly articulated. The document demonstrates a thorough approach to design.
current_prd.architect_reasoning: The Data Model is good. The 'Mermaid ERD Diagram' (high priority) is present, syntactically correct, and shows a reasonable number of entities and relationships, indicating a solid understanding of the data structure. The 'Sample Data' (medium priority) is also present and provides examples for multiple tables with realistic rows. However, the sample data could be more extensive to cover all entities and demonstrate more complex relationships or edge cases. The ERD is good but could be slightly more detailed in terms of attribute types for all entities.
current_prd.product_manager_reasoning: The User Stories section is excellent. There are a large number of user stories (well over the minimum of 5), covering a wide range of functionalities and user types. Each user story is well-structured with clear 'as_a', 'i_want', 'so_that' components and detailed 'acceptance_criteria'. The stories demonstrate good coverage across core productivity features, admin functionalities, and technical aspects (APIs, quick add robustness). The inclusion of 'key_feature' and 'phase' further enhances the organization and completeness.

class TodoList < ApplicationRecord
  belongs_to :organization
  belongs_to :user

  has_many :todo_items, dependent: :destroy

  attribute :visibility, :integer
  enum :visibility, { private_list: 0, public_list: 1, shared_list: 2 }

  validates :name, presence: true, uniqueness: { scope: :organization_id }

  rhino_owner :organization
  rhino_references [:organization, :user]
end
planned.migration_content:
class CreateTodoLists < ActiveRecord::Migration[7.2]
  def change
    create_table :todo_lists do |t|
      t.string :name, null: false
      t.text :description
      t.integer :visibility, default: 0
      t.integer :position
      t.references :organization, null: false, foreign_key: true
      t.references :user, null: false, foreign_key: true

      t.timestamps
    end

    add_index :todo_lists, [:name, :organization_id], unique: true
  end
end
planned.rails_model_generation_command_line: rails generate model TodoList name:string description:text visibility:integer position:integer organization:references user:references

class TodoItem < ApplicationRecord
  belongs_to :organization
  belongs_to :user
  belongs_to :todo_list

  has_many :notifications, dependent: :destroy
  has_many :todo_comments, dependent: :destroy
  has_many :reminders, dependent: :destroy

  attribute :priority, :integer
  enum :priority, { low: 0, medium: 1, high: 2, urgent: 3 }

  validates :title, presence: true

  rhino_owner :organization
  rhino_references [:organization, :user, :todo_list]
end

      t.date :due_date

end
